summary: Zero library candidates — every customer-readiness gap is a defect, a config default, a missing markdown file, or absent infrastructure, none library-shaped. Tier-4 web research (4 queries) returned no actionable product-specific intelligence and is recorded at LOW confidence; the load-bearing evidence is all from the repository. Two new CRITICAL findings beyond the assessment: (1) the default change source FabricChangeSource::watch() returns Ok(empty_stream), so GraphQL subscriptions silently deliver zero events for every deployment path except Helm — it fails open into silence; (2) governance files SECURITY.md/SUPPORT.md/CONTRIBUTING.md/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md are all absent, so there is no vulnerability intake channel. Spec is BLOCKED on one question: what is being sold, and to whom?
key_verdicts: pg_cron amd64 link fix — BUILD, 1 line, blocks integration CI + multi-arch image + operator docs
realtime fail-open default — BUILD, ~10 lines, invert default to 'listen' or return Err instead of Ok(empty)
SECURITY.md — ADOPT OpenSSF CVD template, hours
backup/restore/PITR runbook — BUILD, days, no procedure exists anywhere in-tree
k6 load validation — DEFER, blocked on staging infrastructure that does not exist
corrections_made: Initially mis-flagged 7 todo!() + 1 unimplemented!() as live stubs; all are inside comments/doc-strings. Zero todo!() on any live path — the codebase is cleaner than the grep suggested.

Initially reported subscriptions as universally broken; ListenChangeSource is a complete working LISTEN/NOTIFY implementation and Helm values.yaml:55 sets FLINT_CHANGE_SOURCE=listen. The defect is narrower: the DEFAULT (and .env.example, and docker-compose) fails open to an empty stream.
doc-comment at fdb-reflection/src/compilers/rest/mod.rs:62 claims CRUD handlers are todo!() stubs; they are implemented in mutations.rs. The comment understates the product.
open_questions: BLOCKING: What is being sold, and to whom? Self-hosted OSS vs managed SaaS vs licensed on-prem have disjoint readiness bars; Tier-1/Tier-2 composition depends on the answer.
Does the beta include realtime subscriptions? If yes, OQ-FRF-1 (open since p3) needs a resolution date. If no, remove them from README.
Is amd64 required, or is arm64-only acceptable? (carried from assess)
Who receives a vulnerability report today? No address exists in the repo.
Does a backup/restore procedure exist outside this repository?
Re-tag v1.0.0 or ship v1.0.1? (carried from assess, still unanswered)
next_action: DO NOT run /kbd-spec yet — blocked on the delivery-model question. Independent of that answer, fix pg_cron amd64, fix the realtime fail-open default, and get one green integration run.
